message:
fix(http): prevent state corruption in OutgoingBodySender on cancelled sends

Fix two state-corruption bugs in OutgoingBodySender where internal
bookkeeping was mutated before the actual frame send succeeded,
leaving the sender in an inconsistent state on cancellation or error.

Bug 1 — send_bytes (Content-Length mode):
Previously, `remaining` was decremented and `total_bytes` was advanced
before calling send_frame(). If send_frame returned an error (e.g.,
BodyCancelled), the byte counters were already consumed even though no
data was actually sent. On retry, the sender would either reject
legitimate data (remaining too low) or misreport total_bytes.

Fix: Move the Content-Length overflow check and send_frame() call before
any state mutation. Only decrement remaining and advance total_bytes
after send_frame succeeds.

Bug 2 — send_trailers:
Previously, `self.finished = true` was set before send_frame(). If
trailers send was cancelled, the sender was permanently marked finished
even though trailers were never delivered, making the sender unusable
for retry.

Fix: Set `self.finished = true` only after send_frame() succeeds.

Update existing test (outgoing_body_send_cancelled):
- Use separate Cx instances for the channel and the cancellation to
  avoid cancelling the channel's own context.

Add 2 new regression tests:
- outgoing_body_send_cancelled_does_not_consume_state: verify that
  remaining, total_bytes, and finished are untouched after a cancelled
  send_bytes call on a Content-Length sender
- outgoing_body_send_trailers_cancelled_does_not_finish_sender: verify
  that a cancelled send_trailers leaves the sender usable (not finished,
  sender channel still open), and a subsequent send_bytes succeeds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
